seo.title: Building AI Chatbot with Vercel AI SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai chatbot with Vercel AI SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building AI chatbots with Vercel AI SDK enables seamless streaming UIs and conversational agents using React Server Components. However, complex multi-turn conversations with RAG, intent classification, and tool calls introduce risks like hallucinations, PII leaks, and incoherent responses. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify response accuracy, safety, and coherence before reaching users.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Chatbot Architecture with Vercel AI SDK
architectureOverview.description:
The typical Vercel AI SDK chatbot architecture uses API routes or Server Actions to handle streaming conversations. User messages trigger intent classification, RAG retrieval from vector stores, and LLM calls via generateText or streamText. Multi-turn memory is managed through session state or databases, with tool calls for external actions like database queries or API integrations.

Edge functions process requests with providers like OpenAI or Anthropic, streaming tokens back to React components via useChat hook. Common patterns include structured output extraction for intent/tools and partial response accumulation for real-time display. Failures occur when unvalidated tool results display directly or streaming bypasses safety checks.

Vex integrates as middleware to intercept generateText/streamText calls, verifying outputs against accuracy, safety, and coherence before forwarding to the UI.

risks.name: Streaming responses with partial hallucinations
risks.description: Partial token streams display unverified content containing factual errors before full response completion
risks.mitigation: Vex stream verification buffers and validates partial responses before UI rendering

risks.name: Tool call results not validated before display
risks.description: Unverified tool outputs containing malicious payloads or incorrect data displayed directly to users
risks.mitigation: Vex tool result validation ensures accuracy and safety before response incorporation

risks.name: Inconsistent structured output across providers
risks.description: Intent classification and tool schemas fail across different LLM providers due to varying structured output reliability
risks.mitigation: Vex normalizes and verifies structured outputs regardless of provider implementation

risks.name: Edge function timeouts on complex agent tasks
risks.description: Multi-step reasoning chains exceed Vercel edge function timeouts, causing incomplete conversations
risks.mitigation: Vex task decomposition and parallel verification prevents timeout cascades
